name: Bounded Burnside Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The bounding exponent relation x^n = 1 that restricts the infinite free group into the periodic Burnside group B(m,n).

VINIT.justification: The void state before any generators are multiplied; the uninitialized free group.

TANCH.justification: The hermetic seal x^n=1 that closes the infinite free group into a periodic structure.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism traversing the Cayley graph away from the identity.

AREV.justification: Reverse morphism traversing the Cayley graph back toward the identity.

CLINK.justification: Sequential composition of group elements (morphisms) to form longer words.

IMSCRIB.justification: Self-recognition when a word fully reduces to the identity element, knowing itself as the void.

FSPLIT.justification: Splits an arbitrary word into periodic segments (elementary periods) and non-periodic bridges.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the original word exactly by concatenating its periodic and non-periodic segments.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative state where the bounded exponent forces the group to be finite (small n) or a segment is strictly periodic.

EVALF.justification: Negative state where the group remains infinite despite the bounded exponent (large n) or a segment is aperiodic.

ENGAGR.justification: The open problem state for intermediate n (e.g., n=5), holding both finite and infinite possibilities simultaneously without resolution.

IFIX.justification: The irreversible mathematical proof fixing the existence of infinite groups for large odd n into the permanent record.

phase_2.split_input: Arbitrary group word w in B(m,n)
phase_2.fuse_result: Arbitrary group word w in B(m,n)

phase_3.void_description: The empty word e before any generators are introduced; the uninitialized free group.
phase_3.true_description: The group is finite; the word problem is decidable and all elements have bounded order (small n).
phase_3.false_description: The group is infinite; there exist elements of infinite order in the free group modulo x^n=1 (large n).
phase_3.both_description: The intermediate exponent state (e.g., n=5); the finiteness of B(m,n) is unknown, holding both finite and infinite topologies in paradice.

steps.domain_action: Genesis of the free group F_m from the empty word identity element.
Forward morphism applying right-multiplication by generators to build words.
Reverse morphism applying right-multiplication by inverses to allow reduction.
Sequential composition concatenating generators and inverses into arbitrary free group words.
Imposition of the bounding exponent relation x^n=1, sealing the free group into the Burnside group B(m,n).
Adian's canonical decomposition splitting an arbitrary word w into periodic segments and non-periodic bridges.
Affirmative evaluation of periodic segments, validating local finiteness and strict adherence to the exponent bound.
Negative evaluation of non-periodic bridges, identifying aperiodic residuals that evade local finiteness.
Canonical word reconstruction reconstituting the original word w by concatenating its periodic and non-periodic segments.
Self-recognition of the identity element when a word fully reduces to the empty word e.
The intermediate exponent paradox holding both finite and infinite group topologies simultaneously for unresolved n.
The Novikov-Adian theorem publication permanently fixing the existence of infinite Burnside groups for large odd n.
Final self-reference closing the mathematical proof as a complete, recognized object in the algebraic canon.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Mathematical logic and formal proof systems translating group axioms into theorems.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Algebraic homomorphisms and quotient maps communicating structure between free and Burnside groups.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The Cayley graph and the set of all reduced words storing the group's state.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The word reduction algorithm (e.g., Adian's hierarchy of periods) determining evaluation order.
phase_5.alfs_store: The axioms of group theory and the specific Burnside relator x^n=1.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Computational complexity of the word problem and the length of the reduced word.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: An unreduced, arbitrarily long word in the free group.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A canonically reduced word in the Burnside group B(m,n).
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The reduction process is information-conservative; the equivalence class of the word is perfectly preserved modulo the Burnside relators.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → AREV → CLINK → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → FFUSE → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → IFIX → IMSCRIB
-- Class: Bounded Burnside Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(7,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=13
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑗  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑾 → 𐑱  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [3] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑗 → 𐑡  | composition — regime coherence
--   [4] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑱 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑠  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑙 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑠  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑭 → 𐑼  | identity — self-imscription

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def bounded_burnside_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Genesis of the free group F_m from the empty word identity element.)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑗)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Forward morphism applying right-multiplication by generators to build words.)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑾 𐑱)  -- [2] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Reverse morphism applying right-multiplication by inverses to allow reduction.)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑗 𐑡)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Sequential composition concatenating generators and inverses into arbitrary f...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑚)  -- [4] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Imposition of the bounding exponent relation x^n=1, sealing the free group in...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Adian's canonical decomposition splitting an arbitrary word w into periodic segments and non-periodic bridges.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Affirmative evaluation of periodic segments, validating local finiteness and ...)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Negative evaluation of non-periodic bridges, identifying aperiodic residuals ...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Canonical word reconstruction reconstituting the original word w by concatenating its periodic and non-periodic segments.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [9] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Self-recognition of the identity element when a word fully reduces to the emp...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑭)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (The intermediate exponent paradox holding both finite and infinite group topo...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑠)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (The Novikov-Adian theorem publication permanently fixing the existence of inf...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [12] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Final self-reference closing the mathematical proof as a complete, recognized...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def bounded_burnside_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (bounded_burnside_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def bounded_burnside_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (bounded_burnside_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem bounded_burnside_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
